source_of_acquisition: Chen Zhi fraud seizure (130,000 BTC in 2025)
United States.notes: World's largest disclosed government holder. Executive order ended automatic liquidation policy.

China.notes: On-chain analysis suggests tokens moved to mixers then centralized exchanges - likely liquidated. Official position unclear.

United Kingdom.notes: Policy discussions ongoing about whether to sell or hold long-term.

Bhutan.notes: Secretive sovereign mining operation using abundant hydroelectric power.

El Salvador.notes: First country to adopt Bitcoin as legal tender. Split holdings across 15 wallets citing quantum computing concerns.

Germany.notes: Sold entire holdings at ~$57,900 average. Missed $2.35B in gains as Bitcoin rose 80% by May 2025.

Ukraine.notes: Received significant crypto donations; some converted to fiat for military needs.

total_disclosed.notes: Approximate total of all disclosed government holdings globally

estimated_secret_holdings.methodology: Estimates based on hashrate control, sanctions evasion needs, documented state mining, and intelligence reports

evidence: Estimated 1.47M BTC mined by Chinese entities in that period
State-controlled mining likely 2-5% of Chinese operations
Underground mining continues at 15-25% of global hashrate
Mining pools (AntPool, F2Pool, ViaBTC) still Chinese-controlled
China.estimation_methodology: Conservative 2-5% of Chinese mining was state-controlled; continued covert operations

evidence: Excess energy from gas flaring and stranded hydro
2024 legislation legitimizing and requiring reporting of mining
Treasury sanctioned 13 blockchain firms in March 2025
Russia.estimation_methodology: State entities mining 4-7% of global hashrate; accumulated over several years

evidence: Electricity cost $0.01-0.05/kWh enables $1,300 mining cost per BTC
Iran.estimation_methodology: State controls significant portion of Iran's 4.2% hashrate; accumulated since 2019

evidence: Bybit hack Feb 2025: Stole $1.5B ETH, converted 13,518 BTC
Third largest government holder after Bybit conversion
North Korea.estimation_methodology: Documented on-chain holdings from Lazarus Group operations tracked by Arkham

evidence: Petro cryptocurrency failure led to Bitcoin pivot
Venezuela.estimation_methodology: Limited evidence; speculation based on sanctions evasion needs

United States (undisclosed).estimation_methodology: Speculation; intelligence agencies historically acquire assets covertly

total_estimated_secret.notes: Wide range reflects high uncertainty in covert holdings estimates

comparison_to_corporate.notes: Government holdings (disclosed + estimated) likely exceed all corporate holdings
